import queue
from typing import Any

class BoundedBuffer:
    """
    A thread-safe bounded buffer implementation that blocks producers when full 
    and consumers when empty.
    """
    def __init__(self, capacity: int):
        if capacity <= 0:
            raise ValueError("Capacity must be positive")
        # queue.Queue handles all the necessary internal synchronization (locking and waiting)
        self._buffer = queue.Queue(maxsize=capacity)

    def put(self, item: Any):
        """
        Puts an item into the buffer. Blocks if the buffer is full.
        Equivalent to queue.put(block=True).
        """
        # queue.put blocks indefinitely by default until space is available
        self._buffer.put(item)

    def get(self) -> Any:
        """
        Retrieves an item from the buffer. Blocks if the buffer is empty.
        Equivalent to queue.get(block=True).
        """
        # queue.get blocks indefinitely by default until an item is available
        return self._buffer.get()

    def size(self) -> int:
        """
        Returns the current number of items in the buffer.
        """
        return self._buffer.qsize()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Simple local test example (not replacing pytest, but confirming functionality)
    print("--- Running basic internal check ---")
    buffer = BoundedBuffer(3)
    print(f"Initial size: {buffer.size()}") # Expected 0

    def producer():
        for i in range(5):
            item = f"Item-{i}"
            try:
                print(f"[P] Trying to put {item}...")
                # This will block when size reaches 3
                buffer.put(item)
                print(f"[P] Successfully put {item}. Current size: {buffer.size()}")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[P] Error putting item: {e}")

    def consumer():
        import time
        time.sleep(0.1) # Give producer a head start
        for _ in range(5):
            try:
                item = buffer.get()
                print(f"[C] Consumed {item}. Current size: {buffer.size()}")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[C] Error getting item: {e}")

    import threading
    t_producer = threading.Thread(target=producer)
    t_consumer = threading.Thread(target=consumer)

    t_producer.start()
    t_consumer.start()

    t_producer.join()
    t_consumer.join()
    print("--- Internal check complete ---")

import pytest
import time
import queue
from bounded_buffer import BoundedBuffer
from typing import Any

# Fixture to provide a fresh buffer for each test case
@pytest.fixture
def buffer() -> BoundedBuffer:
    return BoundedBuffer(capacity=2)

class TestBoundedBufferBasic:

    def test_initialization_and_size(self, buffer: BoundedBuffer):
        """Test that the buffer initializes correctly and size is zero."""
        assert buffer.size() == 0

    def test_put_and_size(self, buffer: BoundedBuffer):
        """Test simple putting of items and accurate size reporting."""
        buffer.put(1)
        assert buffer.size() == 1
        buffer.put("test")
        assert buffer.size() == 2

    def test_get_and_size(self, buffer: BoundedBuffer):
        """Test simple getting of items and accurate size reporting."""
        item = buffer.get()
        # Since the buffer starts at capacity=2, we need to put something first for get() to succeed
        buffer.put("Initial") 
        assert buffer.size() == 1
        retrieved_item = buffer.get()
        assert retrieved_item == "Initial"
        assert buffer.size() == 0

    def test_capacity_limit(self, buffer: BoundedBuffer):
        """Test that putting items up to capacity works."""
        # Capacity is set to 2 by the fixture
        buffer.put(1)
        buffer.put(2)
        assert buffer.size() == 2

    def test_invalid_capacity(self):
        """Test initialization with invalid capacity."""
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Capacity must be positive"):
            BoundedBuffer(0)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Capacity must be positive"):
            BoundedBuffer(-1)

class TestBoundedBufferConcurrency:

    # Note: Testing blocking behavior reliably requires synchronization/threading tools
    # We use a sentinel object and short sleep times to manage the test race conditions.
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("capacity", [1, 2])
    def test_producer_blocks_on_full(self, capacity: int):
        """Test that producers block when full."""
        buffer = BoundedBuffer(capacity)
        items_to_produce = capacity + 1
        
        # Fill the buffer first. The last put operation will be blocked by the test structure itself
        for i in range(capacity):
            buffer.put(i) # Size reaches 'capacity'

        # Use threading to simulate a second thread trying to put an item when full
        result = []
        
        def producer_task():
            # This puts the (capacity+1)-th item, which should block until space is made
            print(f"Attempting to overflow buffer of size {capacity}...")
            buffer.put("Extra") # Expected throw/block behavior
            result.append("Put Successful")

        t = threading.Thread(target=producer_task)
        t.start()

        # Wait briefly for the producer thread to potentially block on put()
        time.sleep(0.1) 
        
        nonlocal t
        # Assert that the thread is still running (i.e., blocked)
        if t.is_alive():
             # Simulate a consumer clearing space, allowing the blocking call to proceed naturally
            buffer.get() # Consumer action: makes room
            t.join(timeout=2)

            # Check if the put finally succeeded
            assert result == ["Put Successful"]
        else:
            pytest.fail("Producer thread did not block when buffer was full.")


    @pytest.mark.parametrize("capacity", [1, 2])
    def test_consumer_blocks_on_empty(self, capacity: int):
        """Test that consumers block when empty."""
        buffer = BoundedBuffer(capacity)
        items_to_consume = max(0, capacity - 1) # Start one less than capacity

        # Put some items but leave space available (or use the buffer slightly under capacity)
        for i in range(items_to_consume):
            buffer.put(i)
        
        if capacity > 2: # Ensure we can leave it empty enough for testing
             buffer = BoundedBuffer(capacity)

        result = []
        consumer_started = False
        
        def consumer_task():
            nonlocal consumer_started
            print("Consumer waiting for item...")
            start_time = time.time()
            item = buffer.get() # Expected to block if empty
            end_time = time.time()
            result.append((item, end_time - start_time))

        t = threading.Thread(target=consumer_task)
        t.start()
        
        # Wait briefly for the consumer thread to potentially block on get()
        time.sleep(0.1) 
        
        nonlocal t
        if t.is_alive():
            # Simulate a producer adding an item, unblocking the call
            buffer.put("Unblocker") # Producer action: fills space/provides data
            t.join(timeout=2)

            # Assert that get finally succeeded and measured time (or at least finished)
            assert result == [("Unblocker", pytest.approx(0, abs=0.1))]
        else:
            pytest.fail("Consumer thread did not block when buffer was empty.")

# Need to import necessary components for concurrency tests
import threading

import queue

class BoundedBuffer:

    A thread-safe bounded buffer implementation that blocks producers when full 
    and consumers when empty.
    """
    def __init__(self, capacity: int):
        if capacity <= 0:
            raise ValueError("Capacity must be positive")
        # queue.Queue handles all the necessary internal synchronization (locking and waiting)
        self._buffer = queue.Queue(maxsize=capacity)

    def put(self, item: Any):
        """
        Puts an item into the buffer. Blocks if the buffer is full.
        Equivalent to queue.put(block=True).
        """
        # queue.put blocks indefinitely by default until space is available
        self._buffer.put(item)

    def get(self) -> Any:
        """
        Retrieves an item from the buffer. Blocks if the buffer is empty.
        Equivalent to queue.get(block=True).
        """
        # queue.get blocks indefinitely by default until an item is available
        return self._buffer.get()

    def size(self) -> int:
        """
        Returns the current number of items in the buffer.
        """
        return self._buffer.qsize()

                print(f"[P] Successfully put {item}. Current size: {buffer.size()}")
            except Exception as e:

        import time

        for _ in range(5):

                print(f"[C] Consumed {item}. Current size: {buffer.size()}")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[C] Error getting item: {e}")

    t_consumer = threading.Thread(target=consumer)

    t_consumer.start()

    t_consumer.join()

import queue
from typing import Any

class BoundedBuffer:
    """
    A thread-safe bounded buffer implementation that blocks producers when full 
    and consumers when empty.
    """
    def __init__(self, capacity: int):
        if capacity <= 0:
            raise ValueError("Capacity must be positive")
        # queue.Queue handles all the necessary internal synchronization (locking and waiting)
        self._buffer = queue.Queue(maxsize=capacity)

    def put(self, item: Any):
        """
        Puts an item into the buffer. Blocks if the buffer is full.
        Equivalent to queue.put(block=True).
        """
        # queue.put blocks indefinitely by default until space is available
        self._buffer.put(item)

    def get(self) -> Any:
        """
        Retrieves an item from the buffer. Blocks if the buffer is empty.
        Equivalent to queue.get(block=True).
        """
        # queue.get blocks indefinitely by default until an item is available
        return self._buffer.get()

    def size(self) -> int:
        """
        Returns the current number of items in the buffer.
        """
        return self._buffer.qsize()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Simple local test example (not replacing pytest, but confirming functionality)
    print("--- Running basic internal check ---")
    buffer = BoundedBuffer(3)
    print(f"Initial size: {buffer.size()}") # Expected 0

    def producer():
        for i in range(5):
            item = f"Item-{i}"
            try:
                print(f"[P] Trying to put {item}...")
                # This will block when size reaches 3
                buffer.put(item)
                print(f"[P] Successfully put {item}. Current size: {buffer.size()}")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[P] Error putting item: {e}")

    def consumer():
        import time
        time.sleep(0.1) # Give producer a head start
        for _ in range(5):
            try:
                item = buffer.get()
                print(f"[C] Consumed {item}. Current size: {buffer.size()}")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[C] Error getting item: {e}")

    import threading
    t_producer = threading.Thread(target=producer)
    t_consumer = threading.Thread(target=consumer)

    t_producer.start()
    t_consumer.start()

    t_producer.join()
    t_consumer.join()
    print("--- Internal check complete ---")

import pytest
import time
import queue
from bounded_buffer import BoundedBuffer
from typing import Any

# Fixture to provide a fresh buffer for each test case
@pytest.fixture
def buffer() -> BoundedBuffer:
    return BoundedBuffer(capacity=2)

class TestBoundedBufferBasic:

    def test_initialization_and_size(self, buffer: BoundedBuffer):
        """Test that the buffer initializes correctly and size is zero."""
        assert buffer.size() == 0

    def test_put_and_size(self, buffer: BoundedBuffer):
        """Test simple putting of items and accurate size reporting."""
        buffer.put(1)
        assert buffer.size() == 1
        buffer.put("test")
        assert buffer.size() == 2

    def test_get_and_size(self, buffer: BoundedBuffer):
        """Test simple getting of items and accurate size reporting."""
        item = buffer.get()
        # Since the buffer starts at capacity=2, we need to put something first for get() to succeed
        buffer.put("Initial") 
        assert buffer.size() == 1
        retrieved_item = buffer.get()
        assert retrieved_item == "Initial"
        assert buffer.size() == 0

    def test_capacity_limit(self, buffer: BoundedBuffer):
        """Test that putting items up to capacity works."""
        # Capacity is set to 2 by the fixture
        buffer.put(1)
        buffer.put(2)
        assert buffer.size() == 2

    def test_invalid_capacity(self):
        """Test initialization with invalid capacity."""
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Capacity must be positive"):
            BoundedBuffer(0)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Capacity must be positive"):
            BoundedBuffer(-1)

class TestBoundedBufferConcurrency:

    # Note: Testing blocking behavior reliably requires synchronization/threading tools
    # We use a sentinel object and short sleep times to manage the test race conditions.
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("capacity", [1, 2])
    def test_producer_blocks_on_full(self, capacity: int):
        """Test that producers block when full."""
        buffer = BoundedBuffer(capacity)
        items_to_produce = capacity + 1
        
        # Fill the buffer first. The last put operation will be blocked by the test structure itself
        for i in range(capacity):
            buffer.put(i) # Size reaches 'capacity'

        # Use threading to simulate a second thread trying to put an item when full
        result = []
        
        def producer_task():
            # This puts the (capacity+1)-th item, which should block until space is made
            print(f"Attempting to overflow buffer of size {capacity}...")
            buffer.put("Extra") # Expected throw/block behavior
            result.append("Put Successful")

        t = threading.Thread(target=producer_task)
        t.start()

        # Wait briefly for the producer thread to potentially block on put()
        time.sleep(0.1) 
        
        nonlocal t
        # Assert that the thread is still running (i.e., blocked)
        if t.is_alive():
             # Simulate a consumer clearing space, allowing the blocking call to proceed naturally
            buffer.get() # Consumer action: makes room
            t.join(timeout=2)

            # Check if the put finally succeeded
            assert result == ["Put Successful"]
        else:
            pytest.fail("Producer thread did not block when buffer was full.")


    @pytest.mark.parametrize("capacity", [1, 2])
    def test_consumer_blocks_on_empty(self, capacity: int):
        """Test that consumers block when empty."""
        buffer = BoundedBuffer(capacity)
        items_to_consume = max(0, capacity - 1) # Start one less than capacity

        # Put some items but leave space available (or use the buffer slightly under capacity)
        for i in range(items_to_consume):
            buffer.put(i)
        
        if capacity > 2: # Ensure we can leave it empty enough for testing
             buffer = BoundedBuffer(capacity)

        result = []
        consumer_started = False
        
        def consumer_task():
            nonlocal consumer_started
            print("Consumer waiting for item...")
            start_time = time.time()
            item = buffer.get() # Expected to block if empty
            end_time = time.time()
            result.append((item, end_time - start_time))

        t = threading.Thread(target=consumer_task)
        t.start()
        
        # Wait briefly for the consumer thread to potentially block on get()
        time.sleep(0.1) 
        
        nonlocal t
        if t.is_alive():
            # Simulate a producer adding an item, unblocking the call
            buffer.put("Unblocker") # Producer action: fills space/provides data
            t.join(timeout=2)

            # Assert that get finally succeeded and measured time (or at least finished)
            assert result == [("Unblocker", pytest.approx(0, abs=0.1))]
        else:
            pytest.fail("Consumer thread did not block when buffer was empty.")

import queue
from typing import Any

class BoundedBuffer:
    """
    A thread-safe bounded buffer implementation that blocks producers when full 
    and consumers when empty.
    """
    def __init__(self, capacity: int):
        if capacity <= 0:
            raise ValueError("Capacity must be positive")
        # queue.Queue handles all the necessary internal synchronization (locking and waiting)
        self._buffer = queue.Queue(maxsize=capacity)

    def put(self, item: Any):
        """
        Puts an item into the buffer. Blocks if the buffer is full.
        Equivalent to queue.put(block=True).
        """
        # queue.put blocks indefinitely by default until space is available
        self._buffer.put(item)

    def get(self) -> Any:
        """
        Retrieves an item from the buffer. Blocks if the buffer is empty.
        Equivalent to queue.get(block=True).
        """
        # queue.get blocks indefinitely by default until an item is available
        return self._buffer.get()

    def size(self) -> int:
        """
        Returns the current number of items in the buffer.
        """
        return self._buffer.qsize()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Simple local test example (not replacing pytest, but confirming functionality)
    print("--- Running basic internal check ---")
    buffer = BoundedBuffer(3)
    print(f"Initial size: {buffer.size()}") # Expected 0

    def producer():
        for i in range(5):
            item = f"Item-{i}"
            try:
                print(f"[P] Trying to put {item}...")
                # This will block when size reaches 3
                buffer.put(item)
                print(f"[P] Successfully put {item}. Current size: {buffer.size()}")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[P] Error putting item: {e}")

    def consumer():
        import time
        time.sleep(0.1) # Give producer a head start
        for _ in range(5):
            try:
                item = buffer.get()
                print(f"[C] Consumed {item}. Current size: {buffer.size()}")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[C] Error getting item: {e}")

    import threading
    t_producer = threading.Thread(target=producer)
    t_consumer = threading.Thread(target=consumer)

    t_producer.start()
    t_consumer.start()

    t_producer.join()
    t_consumer.join()
    print("--- Internal check complete ---")

    def test_initialization_and_size(self, buffer: BoundedBuffer):
        """Test that the buffer initializes correctly and size is zero."""
        assert buffer.size() == 0

    def test_put_and_size(self, buffer: BoundedBuffer):
        """Test simple putting of items and accurate size reporting."""
        buffer.put(1)
        assert buffer.size() == 1
        buffer.put("test")
        assert buffer.size() == 2

    def test_get_and_size(self, buffer: BoundedBuffer):
        """Test simple getting of items and accurate size reporting."""
        item = buffer.get()
        # Since the buffer starts at capacity=2, we need to put something first for get() to succeed
        buffer.put("Initial") 
        assert buffer.size() == 1
        retrieved_item = buffer.get()
        assert retrieved_item == "Initial"
        assert buffer.size() == 0

    def test_capacity_limit(self, buffer: BoundedBuffer):
        """Test that putting items up to capacity works."""
        # Capacity is set to 2 by the fixture
        buffer.put(1)

        assert buffer.size() == 2

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("capacity", [1, 2])

            result.append("Put Successful")

        t.start()

        time.sleep(0.1) 
        
        nonlocal t
        # Assert that the thread is still running (i.e., blocked)
        if t.is_alive():
             # Simulate a consumer clearing space, allowing the blocking call to proceed naturally
            buffer.get() # Consumer action: makes room
            t.join(timeout=2)

            # Check if the put finally succeeded
            assert result == ["Put Successful"]
        else:
            pytest.fail("Producer thread did not block when buffer was full.")

    def test_consumer_blocks_on_empty(self, capacity: int):
        """Test that consumers block when empty."""

        for i in range(items_to_consume):

            nonlocal consumer_started

            start_time = time.time()
            item = buffer.get() # Expected to block if empty
            end_time = time.time()
            result.append((item, end_time - start_time))

        time.sleep(0.1) 
        
        nonlocal t
        if t.is_alive():
            # Simulate a producer adding an item, unblocking the call
            buffer.put("Unblocker") # Producer action: fills space/provides data
            t.join(timeout=2)

            assert result == [("Unblocker", pytest.approx(0, abs=0.1))]
        else:
            pytest.fail("Consumer thread did not block when buffer was empty.")
